memberdef.type: typename detail::detector< void, void, Operation, Arguments... >::value_t
memberdef.definition: using scl::is_detected = typedef typename detail::detector<void, void, Operation, Arguments...>::value_t
memberdef.name: is_detected

briefdescription.para: Detects whether Operation<Arguments...> is a valid expression.

detaileddescription.para:
Operation
Template to instantiate
Arguments
Arguments for the template
Alias to std::true_type if Operation<Arguments...> is well-formed, std::false_type otherwise.

memberdef.type: typename detail::detector< void, void, Operation, Arguments... >::type
memberdef.definition: using scl::detected_t = typedef typename detail::detector<void, void, Operation, Arguments...>::type
memberdef.name: detected_t

briefdescription.para: Gets the type of Operation<Arguments...> or void if ill-formed.

memberdef.definition: using scl::detected_or = typedef detail::detector<Default, void, Operation, Arguments...>
memberdef.name: detected_or

briefdescription.para: Helper for getting detected type or a default.

detaileddescription.para:
Default
Type to use if detection fails
Operation
Template to instantiate
Arguments
Arguments for the template
Provides nested type 'type' which is either Operation<Arguments...> if well-formed, or Default otherwise.

memberdef.definition: using scl::detected_or_t = typedef typename detected_or<Default, Operation, Arguments...>::type
memberdef.name: detected_or_t

briefdescription.para: Gets the type of Operation<Arguments...> or Default if ill-formed.

parameterdescription.para: Type to use if detection fails

briefdescription.para: Checks if Operation<Arguments...> results in exactly Expected type.
Checks if Operation<Arguments... > is convertible to To.
Make a type Type "look like" Base for cv-ref qualifiers (matches ::std::forward_like rules).

detaileddescription.para:
Base
Type providing qualifiers/ref to propagate.
Type
Type to which qualifiers/ref are applied.
Semantics:
CV-qualifiers: Applies const and volatile from Base onto remove_reference_t<Type>. (i.e. union of qualifiers, not replacement)
Reference: Applies reference category of Base (if any). If Base is lvalue ref, result is lvalue ref; if rvalue ref, rvalue ref; else no reference. Reference is omitted for void.

briefdescription.para: Pointer-to-member-function type for class Type and function Signature, where the member function's cv/ref qualifiers are copied from Type.

parameterdescription.para: Object type whose cv/ref qualifiers will be mirrored onto the member function. The class type itself is decayed with ::::std::remove_cvref_t before forming the pointer-to-member.
Non-static member function signature as a function type, e.g. Result(Arguments...) or Result(Arguments...) noexcept.

briefdescription.para: Pointer-to-data-member type for class Type and member type Member.

detaileddescription.para: Forms the type Member (::std::remove_cvref_t<Type>::*). Object cv/ref qualifiers do not change the pointer type for data members; they only affect access expressions.

briefdescription.para: Generic pointer-to-member type selector for class Type and Member.

detaileddescription.para:
Selects the appropriate pointer-to-member type based on whether Member is a function type.
If Member is a function type like Result(Arguments...) or Result(Arguments...) noexcept, this aliases to scl::member_function_like_t<Type, Member>, producing: Result (::std::remove_cvref_t<Type>::*)(Arguments...) [cv] [ref] [noexcept] where:
[cv]/[ref] are mirrored from Type (const/volatile and &/&&),
[noexcept] is mirrored from T.
Otherwise, it aliases to scl::member_property_like_t<Type, Member>, producing: Member (::std::remove_cvref_t<Type>::*) (see member_property_like_t for details on how object qualifiers may affect Member).

parameterdescription.para: Class (object) type whose cv/ref qualifiers are mirrored for member functions. The class itself is decayed with ::std::remove_cvref_t before forming the pointer type.
Either a function type (for member functions) or a non-function type (for data members).

briefdescription.para: A helper utility to select a specific overload of a function or member function.

detaileddescription.para: This template class provides a set of call operators that disambiguate overloaded functions based on their argument types (Args...) and qualifiers (const, volatile, lvalue/rvalue references, and noexcept).

parameterdescription.para: The exact argument types used to identify the desired overload.

briefdescription.para: Forwards a value with the cv-ref qualifiers of Base applied to T's type, matching ::std::forward_like semantics.

detaileddescription.para: Example: forward_like<const T&, x>(some_val) will forward some_val as a const lvalue reference if T is const.

memberdef.name: SCL_FUNCTION_DETECTION
memberdef.initializer: template <typename... Arguments> \ using name##_function_operation = decltype(name(::std::declval<Arguments>()...)); \ template <typename... Arguments> \ inline constexpr auto has_##name##_function_v = \ ::scl::is_detected_v<name##_function_operation, Arguments...>; \ template <typename... Arguments> \ using name##_function_t = ::scl::detected_t<name##_function_operation, Arguments...>; \ \ template <typename... Arguments> \ using name##_function_exact_operation = decltype(( \ static_cast<name##_function_operation<Arguments...> (*)(Arguments...)>(&name))( \ ::std::declval<Arguments>()...)); \ template <typename... Arguments> \ inline constexpr auto has_##name##_function_exact_v = \ ::scl::is_detected_v<name##_function_exact_operation, Arguments...>; \ template <typename... Arguments> \ using name##_function_exact_t = ::scl::detected_t<name##_function_exact_operation, Arguments...>;

briefdescription.para: Generates a suite of traits to detect the existence of a specific free function.

detaileddescription.para: For a given name foo, this macro generates:

listitem.para:
Basic Detection (Supports ADL):
foo_function_operation<Args...>: The detection alias using foo(declval<Args>()...).
has_foo_function_v<Args...>: Boolean constant.
foo_function_t<Args...>: Resulting return type.

Exact Detection (Checks for a specific symbol pointer):
foo_function_exact_operation<Args...>: Uses &foo to verify a specific function exists.
has_foo_function_exact_v<Args...>: Boolean constant.
foo_function_exact_t<Args...>: Resulting return type.

parameterdescription.para: The name of the function to detect.

simplesect.para: CRITICAL: This macro MUST be invoked AFTER the target function has been declared. Since the macro expansion takes the address of the function (&name), the symbol must be visible to the compiler at the point of expansion, or a compilation error will occur.

briefdescription.para: Generates a suite of traits to detect the existence of a specific member method.

detaileddescription.para: For a given name foo, this macro generates:

listitem.para:
Basic Detection (Checks if val.foo(args...) is valid):
foo_method_operation<Type, Args...>: The detection alias.
has_foo_method_v<Type, Args...>: Boolean constant.
foo_method_t<Type, Args...>: Resulting return type (or nonesuch).

Exact Member Detection (Checks &Type::foo existence and ref-qualifiers):
foo_method_exact_operation<Type, Args...>: The exact detection alias.
has_foo_method_exact_v<Type, Args...>: Boolean constant.
foo_method_exact_t<Type, Args...>: Resulting return type.

Basic Static Detection (Checks if Type::foo(args...) is valid):
foo_static_method_operation<Type, Args...>: The detection alias.
has_foo_static_method_v<Type, Args...>: Boolean constant.
foo_static_method_t<Type, Args...>: Resulting return type (or nonesuch).

Exact Static Member Detection (Checks &Type::foo existence):
foo_static_method_exact_operation<Type, Args...>: The exact detection alias.
has_foo_static_method_exact_v<Type, Args...>: Boolean constant.
foo_static_method_exact_t<Type, Args...>: Resulting return type.

parameterdescription.para: The name of the member function to detect.
